title: Quorum-confirmed selection and read repair
prompt: Fix the quorum reader so it selects the greatest record that is itself confirmed by at least the configured number of replicas. Exact records are grouped by both version and value, with version first and value as a deterministic tie-breaker. After a successful selection, repair only successful replicas whose record orders before the selected record. Partial read failures are tolerated when a record still has quorum; a read with no confirmed record returns ErrNoQuorum and performs no repair. If the context is canceled before replica collection completes, return the cancellation and perform no repair. In particular, a higher-version record observed on fewer than quorum replicas is unconfirmed: it must not be returned, propagated to stale replicas, or overwritten by an older confirmed record. Do not modify the protected tests. Run `bash test.sh`; verification remains offline and deterministic.
